title: juglandaceae
intro: The juglandaceae rank is a taxonomic family of flowering plants, commonly known as walnuts and hickories. This family comprises approximately 60 species, primarily native to the Northern Hemisphere. Juglandaceae is characterized by its deciduous trees, often with large, pinnate leaves and edible nuts. The family includes several economically important species, such as English walnut (Juglans regia) and black walnut (Juglans nigra).
